page: Amazon Web Services c8g.large is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, provides no bundled GPUs, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance demonstrates average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1706.89 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1808.0. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression is strong at 14.15 GB/sec, and gzip compression is average at 30.27 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory-intensive operations are generally weak or poor. The stress-ng multi-core score is 3402.99, and the Geekbench multi-core score is 3220.0. Memory bandwidth tests show a weak read-only small block speed of 230.31 GB/sec and a weak write large block speed of 22.16 GB/sec, though read-only large block bandwidth is average at 55.26 GB/sec. Passmark CPU and memory scores are poor, at 1989.17 and 1099.29 respectively.

Given its hardware profile, the c8g.large offers a cost-efficient entry point for arm64-native workloads that require dedicated compute resources but do not demand high memory capacity or massive multi-threaded throughput. It is suitable for lightweight single-threaded tasks, basic database operations, and simple web serving. It is less suitable for memory-intensive databases, software compilation, or large-scale multi-threaded processing due to its low memory allocation and weak multi-core benchmark results.
description: Amazon Web Services c8g.large is an arm64 compute-optimized instance powered by a 2.8 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 4.0 GB of system memory, operating on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, and it provides a 1 Gbps baseline network speed. Benchmarks show average single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 1808.0 and strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 14.15 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are generally weak, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 3220.0 and a passmark memory score of 1099.29. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight, arm64-native workloads, single-threaded applications, and basic database operations, but is constrained for memory-heavy or highly parallelized tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.large is a compute-optimized arm64 instance featuring 2 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 4.0 GB of RAM, and a 1 Gbps network baseline, suitable for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.large is a compute-optimized arm64 instance with 2 dedicated Graviton cores, 4.0 GB RAM, and average single-core performance.
tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores and balanced single-threaded performance for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 arm64 processor with 2 dedicated cores
Equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression and average single-core benchmark results
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks and basic database operations
